# Dansk Kemisortering A/S

> Danish company specializing in sustainable handling of hazardous chemical
> waste — specifically plastic containers with acids and bases collected from
> consumer recycling centers. We sort and recycle packaging materials and
> route acids/bases to specialized treatment, as an alternative to default
> incineration of hazardous waste.

## Overview

- **Legal name:** Dansk Kemisortering A/S
- **CVR (Danish company registry):** 46183231
- **Founded:** 2018
- **Service area:** All of Denmark (nationwide)
- **Headquarters:** P. O. Pedersens Vej 2, 8200 Aarhus N, Denmark
- **Sorting facility (Jutland):** Sønderlundvej 10, 7500 Holstebro, Denmark
- **Phone:** +45 5555 5252
- **Email:** info@kemisortering.dk
- **Website:** https://www.kemisortering.dk/
- **Language:** Danish (da-DK)

## What we do

We receive sealed drums (spændelåsfade) packed with vermiculite from Danish
recycling stations (genbrugspladser). Each drum contains plastic containers
of hazardous chemical waste — typically household acids and bases — sorted
by private consumers. Standard practice in Denmark is to incinerate the
entire drum and its contents. We instead:

1. **Receive** sealed drums packed in vermiculite.
2. **Recycle** packaging materials where possible.
3. **Treat** acids and bases through specialized chemical processing,
   handled separately with thorough cleaning between batches.
4. **Destroy** residual granulate safely.

This is a circular-economy alternative to default incineration, designed to
be environmentally approved (miljøgodkendt), safe, and economically viable.

## Key facts

- Specializes in plastic containers with acids and bases — not general
  hazardous waste.
- Operations are environmentally approved with high safety prioritization.
- Acid and base containers are processed separately, with thorough cleaning
  between batches.
- Ongoing R&D into recycling possibilities for plastic granulate.
- Also supplies UN-approved sealed drums (spændelåsfade) and vermiculite.
